JavaScript tests for `Math.abs` function behavior with `NaN`, negative zero, negative infinity, and negative integers.


## Files
- **[15.08.02.01-001.js](15.08.02.01-001.js.md)**: Tests that `Math.abs` returns `NaN` when given `NaN` as input.
- **[15.08.02.01-002.js](15.08.02.01-002.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of `Math.abs` with negative zero in JavaScript.
- **[15.08.02.01-003.js](15.08.02.01-003.js.md)**: Tests that the absolute value of negative infinity is positive infinity in JavaScript.
- **[15.08.02.01-004.js](15.08.02.01-004.js.md)**: Tests if the absolute value of negative infinity is positive infinity in JavaScript.
- **[15.08.02.01-005.js](15.08.02.01-005.js.md)**: Tests the Math.abs function to verify it returns the absolute value of a negative integer.
